edges.description: Intent Engine persists snapshots via Data Store
Agent Watcher persists branch state via Data Store
Agent Watcher triggers intent extraction on new prompt detection
Capture Engine persists snapshots via Data Store
Capture Engine reads current agent states during snapshot
Event Aggregator reads events and persists briefings/review logs
Event Aggregator subscribes to agent status change events
App Shell routes IPC commands to Data Store for reads
App Shell routes intent refinement commands
App Shell routes agent management commands
App Shell triggers capture on task switch
Task UI invokes Tauri IPC commands via App Shell
Graph View fetches graph data via App Shell IPC

external_dependencies.name: SQLite (via rusqlite)
external_dependencies.purpose: Local persistence for all entities, WAL mode for crash recovery

external_dependencies.name: React Flow (@xyflow/react)
external_dependencies.purpose: Git graph visualization with custom nodes and edges

external_dependencies.name: notify (Rust crate)
external_dependencies.purpose: Cross-platform filesystem watching for Claude Code session monitoring

external_dependencies.name: git2 (libgit2 bindings)
external_dependencies.purpose: Git status, diff summary, branch info without shelling out to git CLI

external_dependencies.name: Tauri 2 framework
external_dependencies.purpose: Application framework: tray, window management, IPC, cross-platform packaging

external_dependencies.name: dagre (via @dagrejs/dagre)
external_dependencies.purpose: Directed graph layout algorithm for intent graph node positioning

external_dependencies.name: active-win (or platform-specific APIs)
external_dependencies.purpose: Detect active window and focused application for environment snapshots
alternatives: macOS Accessibility API + Windows UI Automation direct

ipc_error_format.structure: { code: string, message: string, context?: Record<string, unknown> }

global_error_strategy.external_service_strategy: No external services in MVP (zero network). All errors are local: filesystem access, git operations, window focus detection. Strategy: log error, return graceful degradation result (e.g., partial snapshot), never block user flow.
global_error_strategy.logging: Rust tracing crate with file rotation. Log levels: ERROR (data loss risk), WARN (degraded capture), INFO (state transitions), DEBUG (watcher events). Logs stored in app data directory, rotated at 10MB.

auto_decisions.decision: Use UUID v7 (time-sortable) for all entity IDs instead of auto-increment integers
auto_decisions.rationale: UUID v7 is time-sortable (useful for chronological queries) and globally unique (enables potential future sync). Auto-increment would be simpler but creates tight coupling to SQLite.
auto_decisions.impact: ID format affects all CRUD operations and IPC schemas. Low risk since all IDs are opaque strings in the UI.

auto_decisions.decision: Store environment snapshot sub-fields (git_status, active_files, window_focus, agent_states) as JSON columns in SQLite rather than normalized tables
auto_decisions.rationale: Snapshot data is write-once/read-once (captured on suspend, read on resume). The schema of sub-fields may evolve rapidly in MVP. JSON columns avoid migration overhead while keeping everything in one queryable store.
auto_decisions.impact: Trades query flexibility for schema evolution speed. Acceptable since snapshots are only queried by task_id + latest.

auto_decisions.decision: Use Tauri event system (emit/listen) for Rust→JS event propagation rather than polling from frontend
auto_decisions.rationale: Tauri's built-in event system naturally bridges Rust and JS with typed events. Avoids polling overhead and provides real-time updates. Well-documented in Tauri 2.
auto_decisions.impact: Frontend components subscribe to events (agent.status_changed, capture.completed) rather than polling. Affects all reactive UI components.

auto_decisions.decision: Use dagre for graph layout rather than custom layout algorithm
auto_decisions.rationale: dagre is the standard directed graph layout used with React Flow. Git-graph-like top-down layout is its sweet spot. Custom layout would give more control but at significant development cost. The graph is small enough (typically <30 nodes) that dagre performance is not a concern.
auto_decisions.impact: Graph visual structure is determined by dagre's algorithm. Custom overrides possible via React Flow's node positioning API if dagre defaults don't look right.

auto_decisions.decision: Hybrid agent monitoring: filesystem watch (primary) + periodic poll (fallback) for Claude Code sessions
auto_decisions.rationale: fs watch provides real-time detection of session changes. Periodic poll (every 5s per REQ-004-AC-1) catches events missed by fs watch (e.g., atomic renames, NFS edge cases). Belt-and-suspenders approach is more robust for an unstable target format.
auto_decisions.impact: Slightly higher resource usage than pure watch, but significantly more reliable. The 5s poll interval is negligible on modern hardware.

auto_decisions.decision: Store Briefing as a persisted entity rather than computing on-the-fly from events
alternatives: Compute briefing dynamically from unread events on each request
auto_decisions.rationale: Persisted briefings can be marked as read, have stable IDs, and support 'show me last briefing' queries. Dynamic computation would need to track read/unread state separately.
auto_decisions.impact: Adds a briefings table. Minimal storage overhead since briefings are small JSON blobs.

auto_decisions.decision: Add minimal review log query interface (filter by task_id, date range) to satisfy Critic's finding on REQ-011 consumption path
alternatives: No query interface in MVP (raw SQLite only)
auto_decisions.rationale: Critic flagged REQ-011 has no defined consumption path. A minimal query API (list by task, list by date range) enables basic retrospective use without over-engineering.
auto_decisions.impact: Adds 1-2 IPC commands. No UI impact in MVP (query via developer tools or future UI).
